devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इत्थं पृथुमभिष्टूय रुषा प्रस्फुरिताधरम् । पुनराहावनिर्भीता संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaitthaṃ pṛthumabhiṣṭūya ruṣā prasphuritādharampunarāhāvanirbhītā saṃstabhyātmānamātmanā
translation:
Maitreya said:
Having thus praised Pṛthu whose lips were quivering through rage, the goddess of the earth steadied her mind herself (by her reasoning and will power) and addressed the king again, although she was afraid (of him).

devanagari: सन्नियच्छाभिभो मन्युं निबोध श्रावितं च मे । सर्वतः सारमादत्ते यथा मधुकरो बुधः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sanniyacchābhibho manyuṃ nibodha śrāvitaṃ ca mesarvataḥ sāramādatte yathā madhukaro budhaḥ
translation: “Oh King! Please control your wrath completely.[1] Please consider what I tell you. For like a bee, a wise man takes the essence from everything.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Restrain your anger so as to offer me protection.—(Bhāvāratha Dīpikā).

devanagari: अस्मिन् लोकेऽथवामुष्मिन् मुनिभिः तत्त्वदर्शिभिः । दृष्टा योगाः प्रयुक्ताश्च पुंसां श्रेयःप्रसिद्धये ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: asmin loke'thavāmuṣmin munibhiḥ tattvadarśibhiḥdṛṣṭā yogāḥ prayuktāśca puṃsāṃ śreyaḥprasiddhaye
translation: Expedients for the accomplishments of the welfare of men in this world as well as in the next, have been discovered and employed by the sages who have realized the truth.

devanagari: तानातिष्ठति यः सम्यग् उपायान् पूर्वदर्शितान् । अवरः श्रद्धयोपेत उपेयान् विन्दतेऽञ्जसा ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tānātiṣṭhati yaḥ samyag upāyān pūrvadarśitānavaraḥ śraddhayopeta upeyān vindate'ñjasā
translation: Anyone belonging to the later periods, who properly and with faith adopts the measures demonstrated by the ancients, easily accomplishes his objectives.

devanagari: तान् अनादृत्य योऽविद्वान् अर्थान् आरभते स्वयम् । तस्य व्यभिचरन्त्यर्था आरब्धाश्च पुनः पुनः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tān anādṛtya yo'vidvān arthān ārabhate svayamtasya vyabhicarantyarthā ārabdhāśca punaḥ punaḥ
translation: Even if a learned person,[2] disregarding those expedients adopted and tested successfully by the ancients, tries to accomplish those objectives in his own (independent) ways, his objects are never realized, despite his frequent efforts and experiments.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
If the text is read as avidvān, ‘one who in his ignorance’ etc.—(Bhāvāratha Dīpikā).

devanagari: पुरा सृष्टा ह्योषधयो ब्रह्मणा या विशाम्पते । भुज्यमाना मया दृष्टा असद्भिः अधृतव्रतैः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: purā sṛṣṭā hyoṣadhayo brahmaṇā yā viśāmpatebhujyamānā mayā dṛṣṭā asadbhiḥ adhṛtavrataiḥ
translation: Oh King! It has been observed by me that the herbs and plants which were produced by god Brahmā formerly, were being enjoyed by the wicked who did not undertake religious observances.

devanagari: अपालितानादृता च भवद्भिः लोकपालकैः । चोरीभूतेऽथ लोकेऽहं यज्ञार्थेऽग्रसमोषधीः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: apālitānādṛtā ca bhavadbhiḥ lokapālakaiḥcorībhūte'tha loke'haṃ yajñārthe'grasamoṣadhīḥ
translation: When I was left unprotected and uncared for by rulers of the people like you (i.e. Vena and other ordinary kings), and when the world was infested with thieves, I swallowed up those herbs and plants (to preserve them within me) for the performance of sacrifices.

devanagari: नूनं ता वीरुधः क्षीणा मयि कालेन भूयसा । तत्र योगेन दृष्टेन भवानादातुमर्हति ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ tā vīrudhaḥ kṣīṇā mayi kālena bhūyasātatra yogena dṛṣṭena bhavānādātumarhati
translation: Due to a lapse of a long period, those plants and herbs within me have become diminished and weak. You should, therefore, try to take them out by some expedient known to be effective in such matters.

devanagari: वत्सं कल्पय मे वीर येनाहं वत्सला तव । धोक्ष्ये क्षीरमयान् कामान् अनुरूपं च दोहनम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: vatsaṃ kalpaya me vīra yenāhaṃ vatsalā tavadhokṣye kṣīramayān kāmān anurūpaṃ ca dohanam
translation: Oh mighty-armed hero! Oh protector of creatures! If Your Lordship desires (to provide) nutritious food coveted by creatures, please arrange for a calf, a suitable vessel (milk-pot) and a milk-man competent to draw (my) milk, so that, being full of affection, I shall yield for you all your desired objects in the form of milk, Oh warrior.

devanagari: दोग्धारं च महाबाहो भूतानां भूतभावन । अन्नं ईप्सितमूर्जस्वद् भगवान् वाञ्छते यदि ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: dogdhāraṃ ca mahābāho bhūtānāṃ bhūtabhāvanaannaṃ īpsitamūrjasvad bhagavān vāñchate yadi
translation: Oh mighty-armed hero! Oh protector of creatures! If Your Lordship desires (to provide) nutritious food coveted by creatures, please arrange for a calf, a suitable vessel (milk-pot) and a milk-man competent to draw (my) milk, so that, being full of affection, I shall yield for you all your desired objects in the form of milk, Oh warrior.

devanagari: समां च कुरु मां राजन् देववृष्टं यथा पयः । अपर्तावपि भद्रं ते उपावर्तेत मे विभो ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: samāṃ ca kuru māṃ rājan devavṛṣṭaṃ yathā payaḥapartāvapi bhadraṃ te upāvarteta me vibho
translation: Oh mighty king! Make me a level plain so that the waters showered by god Indra may stand on me, even after the rainy season is over. God may bless you.”

devanagari: इति प्रियं हितं वाक्यं भुव आदाय भूपतिः । वत्सं कृत्वा मनुं पाणौ अदुहत्सकलौषधीः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti priyaṃ hitaṃ vākyaṃ bhuva ādāya bhūpatiḥvatsaṃ kṛtvā manuṃ pāṇau aduhatsakalauṣadhīḥ
translation: Accepting the agreeable and wholesome words of advice of the goddess of the earth, the king made (Svāyam- bhuva) Manu as the calf, and milked all the herbs and plants (such as cereals etc.), in his own hand.

devanagari: तथापरे च सर्वत्र सारमाददते बुधाः । ततोऽन्ये च यथाकामं दुदुहुः पृथुभाविताम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpare ca sarvatra sāramādadate budhāḥtato'nye ca yathākāmaṃ duduhuḥ pṛthubhāvitām
translation: Similarly, other wise men also extracted essence of everything everywhere. Then others (fifteen including sages) drew out their desired objects from the cow (earth) tamed by Pṛthu.

devanagari: ऋषयो दुदुहुर्देवीं इन्द्रियेष्वथ सत्तम । वत्सं बृहस्पतिं कृत्वा पयश्छन्दोमयं शुचि ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣayo duduhurdevīṃ indriyeṣvatha sattamavatsaṃ bṛhaspatiṃ kṛtvā payaśchandomayaṃ śuci
translation: Oh most righteous Vidura! Then the sages made Bṛhaspati, the calf and drew from the goddess (earth) milk in the form of the holy Vedas, into their own sense-organs (viz. ears, speech and mind).

devanagari: कृत्वा वत्सं सुरगणा इन्द्रं सोमं अदूदुहन् । हिरण्मयेन पात्रेण वीर्यमोजो बलं पयः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtvā vatsaṃ suragaṇā indraṃ somaṃ adūduhanhiraṇmayena pātreṇa vīryamojo balaṃ payaḥ
translation: The gods made Indra a calf, and extracted in a vessel of gold Soma-juice, and (nectarine) milk giving mental vigour, splendour, energy and physical strength.

devanagari: दैतेया दानवा वत्सं प्रह्लादं असुरर्षभम् । विधायादूदुहन् क्षीरमयःपात्रे सुरासवम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: daiteyā dānavā vatsaṃ prahlādaṃ asurarṣabhamvidhāyādūduhan kṣīramayaḥpātre surāsavam
translation: (The demonic tribes) Daityas and Dānavas made Prahlāda, the foremost among the Asuras, as the calf, and milked from her wine and decoction (spirituous liquors) in a vessel of iron.

devanagari: गन्धर्वाप्सरसोऽधुक्षन् पात्रे पद्ममये पयः । वत्सं विश्वावसुं कृत्वा गान्धर्वं मधु सौभगम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: gandharvāpsaraso'dhukṣan pātre padmamaye payaḥvatsaṃ viśvāvasuṃ kṛtvā gāndharvaṃ madhu saubhagam
translation: (The celestial artistes), Gandharvas (heavenly musicians) and Apsaras (heavenly nymphs) made Viśvāvasu the calf, and extracted the milk in the cup of lotus-flower and it became the special Gāndharva honey giving sweetness of voice and beauty.

devanagari: वत्सेन पितरोऽर्यम्णा कव्यं क्षीरमधुक्षत । आमपात्रे महाभागाः श्रद्धया श्राद्धदेवताः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: vatsena pitaro'ryamṇā kavyaṃ kṣīramadhukṣataāmapātre mahābhāgāḥ śraddhayā śrāddhadevatāḥ
translation: The venerable manes (Pitṛs), the presiding deities of the Śrāddha ceremony performed in memory of the departed souls, made Aryaman (the head of the Pitṛs) as the calf, and reverentially drew milk in an unbaked earthen vessel and it became Kavya (food for the manes).

devanagari: प्रकल्प्य वत्सं कपिलं सिद्धाः सङ्कल्पनामयीम् । सिद्धिं नभसि विद्यां च ये च विद्याधरादयः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: prakalpya vatsaṃ kapilaṃ siddhāḥ saṅkalpanāmayīmsiddhiṃ nabhasi vidyāṃ ca ye ca vidyādharādayaḥ
translation: The Siddhas (Semi-divine beings) made Kapila the calf, and extracted in the vessel of the sky the eight superhuman powers (Siddhis) exercisable by mere will. The Vidyādharas (a tribe of demigods) drew the milk in the form of the art of moving through the sky (in the same vessel with Kapila as the calf).

devanagari: अन्ये च मायिनो मायां अन्तर्धानाद्‍भुतात्मनाम् । मयं प्रकल्प्य वत्सं ते दुदुहुर्धारणामयीम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: anye ca māyino māyāṃ antardhānād‍bhutātmanāmmayaṃ prakalpya vatsaṃ te duduhurdhāraṇāmayīm
translation: Others (such as Kimpuruṣas, a semi-divine tribe) noted for their power of conjuring tricks (māyins) made Maya the calf and got from her, magical powers possessed by wonderful beings who can become invisible at will.

devanagari: यक्षरक्षांसि भूतानि पिशाचाः पिशिताशनाः । भूतेशवत्सा दुदुहुः कपाले क्षतजासवम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yakṣarakṣāṃsi bhūtāni piśācāḥ piśitāśanāḥbhūteśavatsā duduhuḥ kapāle kṣatajāsavam
translation: The Yakṣas and Rākṣasas (demonic beings), Bhūtas and Piśācas (ghosts and fiends) who feed upon raw flesh made the Lord of ghosts (Rudra) as the calf, and extracted in a skull the wine of blood.

devanagari: तथाहयो दन्दशूकाः सर्पा नागाश्च तक्षकम् । विधाय वत्सं दुदुहुः बिलपात्रे विषं पयः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāhayo dandaśūkāḥ sarpā nāgāśca takṣakamvidhāya vatsaṃ duduhuḥ bilapātre viṣaṃ payaḥ
translation: In the same way, hoodless reptiles, scorpions, serpents and snakes made Takṣaka (the chief of Nāgas) a calf, and drew poison as the milk, in their mouths as the vessels.

devanagari: पशवो यवसं क्षीरं वत्सं कृत्वा च गोवृषम् । अरण्यपात्रे चाधुक्षन् मृगेन्द्रेण च दंष्ट्रिणः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: paśavo yavasaṃ kṣīraṃ vatsaṃ kṛtvā ca govṛṣamaraṇyapātre cādhukṣan mṛgendreṇa ca daṃṣṭriṇaḥ
translation: Herbivorous beasts made the bull of Rudra the calf and drew grass as their milk, in the vessel in the form of the jungle. The carnivorous beasts with big tusks and feeding upon raw meat, with the lion (the king of beasts) as the calf, drew in (the vessel in the form of) their body flesh (as their milk). The birds who used Garuḍa (the chief of birds) as the calf, had mobiles (like worms and insects) and immobiles (life fruits) for their milk.

devanagari: क्रव्यादाः प्राणिनः क्रव्यं दुदुहुः स्वे कलेवरे । सुपर्णवत्सा विहगाः चरं च अचरमेव च ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: kravyādāḥ prāṇinaḥ kravyaṃ duduhuḥ sve kalevaresuparṇavatsā vihagāḥ caraṃ ca acarameva ca
translation: Herbivorous beasts made the bull of Rudra the calf and drew grass as their milk, in the vessel in the form of the jungle. The carnivorous beasts with big tusks and feeding upon raw meat, with the lion (the king of beasts) as the calf, drew in (the vessel in the form of) their body flesh (as their milk). The birds who used Garuḍa (the chief of birds) as the calf, had mobiles (like worms and insects) and immobiles (life fruits) for their milk.

devanagari: वटवत्सा वनस्पतयः पृथग्रसमयं पयः । गिरयो हिमवद्वत्सा नानाधातून् स्वसानुषु ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: vaṭavatsā vanaspatayaḥ pṛthagrasamayaṃ payaḥgirayo himavadvatsā nānādhātūn svasānuṣu
translation: With the Banyan tree as the calf, the trees extracted their own respective juices as the milk. Mountains had the highest (mountain) Himālaya as the calf and milked various minerals (as their milk) in (the vessels in the form of) their ridges.

devanagari: सर्वे स्वमुख्यवत्सेन स्वे स्वे पात्रे पृथक्पयः । सर्वकामदुघां पृथ्वीं दुदुहुः पृथुभाविताम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sarve svamukhyavatsena sve sve pātre pṛthakpayaḥsarvakāmadughāṃ pṛthvīṃ duduhuḥ pṛthubhāvitām
translation: All species of beings used their own chiefs as the calf and extracted severally in their own vessels the milk specifically useful as food to their own species, from the earth which yielded all desired objects when she was tamed by Pṛthu.

devanagari: एवं पृथ्वादयः पृथ्वीं अन्नादाः स्वन्नमात्मनः । दोहवत्सादिभेदेन क्षीरभेदं कुरूद्वह ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ pṛthvādayaḥ pṛthvīṃ annādāḥ svannamātmanaḥdohavatsādibhedena kṣīrabhedaṃ kurūdvaha
translation: Oh Vidura (son of the Kuru family)! In this way, Pṛthu and others who subsisted on food obtained from the earth different kinds of milk in the form of their specific food, using different kinds of calfs and milk-pots.

devanagari: ततो महीपतिः प्रीतः सर्वकामदुघां पृथुः । दुहितृत्वे चकारेमां प्रेम्णा दुहितृवत्सलः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tato mahīpatiḥ prītaḥ sarvakāmadughāṃ pṛthuḥduhitṛtve cakāremāṃ premṇā duhitṛvatsalaḥ
translation: Thereafter Pṛthu, the lord of the earth, was highly pleased with the earth-goddess who yielded all desired objects, and affectionate as he was to daughters, he regarded her as his daughter, out of paternal affection.

devanagari: चूर्णयन्स्वधनुष्कोट्या गिरिकूटानि राजराट् । भूमण्डलं इदं वैन्यः प्रायश्चक्रे समं विभुः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: cūrṇayansvadhanuṣkoṭyā girikūṭāni rājarāṭbhūmaṇḍalaṃ idaṃ vainyaḥ prāyaścakre samaṃ vibhuḥ
translation: The mighty son of Vena, the king of kings, crushed to pieces mountain-peaks with the ends of his bow and made the surface of the globe of earth almost level.

devanagari: अथास्मिन् भगवान् वैन्यः प्रजानां वृत्तिदः पिता । निवासान् कल्पयां चक्रे तत्र तत्र यथार्हतः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: athāsmin bhagavān vainyaḥ prajānāṃ vṛttidaḥ pitānivāsān kalpayāṃ cakre tatra tatra yathārhataḥ
translation: The illustrious son of Vena was a father unto his subjects, as he provided them with means of livelihood. He then constructed on the earth, at different places, suitable houses for them.

devanagari: ग्रामान्पुरः पत्तनानि दुर्गाणि विविधानि च । घोषान् व्रजान् सशिबिरान् आकरान् खेटखर्वटान् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: grāmānpuraḥ pattanāni durgāṇi vividhāni caghoṣān vrajān saśibirān ākarān kheṭakharvaṭān
translation: He founded a number of villages, towns, cities, forts and castles of different types. He built abodes for cowherds and cowpens and planned camping grounds, quarries. He provided agricultural habitations and hamlets on hillsides.

devanagari: प्राक्पृथोरिह नैवैषा पुरग्रामादिकल्पना । यथासुखं वसन्ति स्म तत्र तत्राकुतोभयाः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: prākpṛthoriha naivaiṣā puragrāmādikalpanāyathāsukhaṃ vasanti sma tatra tatrākutobhayāḥ
translation:
Before the time of Pṛthu, there was no such planning and construction of cities, towns etc. in this world. During his reign subjects began to live comfortably at various places (in rural and urban areas) with a sense of security.
[back to top]
